rationale: Exceptional profile writing with vivid, concrete details (147 open cases, sixty-two cents per dollar, worn carpet, broken printer since March) that do real argumentative work without editorializing. The voice is distinctive and literary—similes like 'the way a person states weather' and 'like a bass note you feel more than hear' are precise and earned. Language is plain and direct throughout; no inflated diction. The piece is clearly incomplete (cuts off mid-sentence), which slightly dings structure/flow since we can't assess the full arc, but what exists moves seamlessly from the personal to the systemic. The Diane punchline is perfectly placed. One of the strongest profile openings I've seen from a prompt like this.
